document: Path 1 — Building a Voice-Enabled AI Chatbot Using Google Gemini API
end_goal: A working voice-enabled AI assistant called Friday deployed live on the internet

friday_features.description: Users type messages and Friday responds intelligently
Friday remembers what was said earlier in the conversation
Friday remembers facts about the user across sessions
Users speak to Friday using a microphone
Friday speaks its responses aloud in a natural voice
Friday searches the internet to answer current questions
Friday reads and answers questions about uploaded PDFs
Friday has a consistent name, tone, and style
Clean polished chat UI accessible from any browser
Friday is live on the internet with a shareable link

tech_stack.purpose: Main programming language for backend and AI logic
The AI brain — understands and responds to messages
Backend web server that connects frontend to AI
Framework to manage AI conversations, memory, and tools
Vector database that stores and retrieves memories
Text-to-speech — converts Friday's response to voice
Packages the entire app so it runs anywhere
Version control — tracks all code changes
Free cloud platform to deploy Friday online
Real-time communication between frontend and backend

team_roles.responsibility: Gemini API, LangChain, memory system, voice integration
RAG pipeline, vector DB, dataset preparation, embeddings
FastAPI server, WebSockets, API endpoints, tool execution
React chat UI, voice controls, design, user experience
Docker, deployment, GitHub Actions, monitoring, reliability

months.title: Foundation and First Working Chatbot
months.goal: Every team member is set up and Friday responds to text messages in a browser.

tasks.detail: Download from python.org, install on every team member's machine
Download from code.visualstudio.com, install Python and Git extensions
Download from git-scm.com, configure with name and email on each machine
Every member creates a free account at github.com
Shuv creates private repo called friday-ai, adds all members as collaborators
All members practice: git clone, add, commit, push, pull
Review: functions, classes, loops, dictionaries, file reading, pip install
Run: pip install requests python-dotenv google-generativeai
Setup: /backend, /frontend, /data, /docs, /tests folders in repo
Set fixed 15-minute daily call time — morning works best — commit to it

weeks.title: Gemini API Setup and First Response

tasks.detail: Go to aistudio.google.com, sign in, click Get API Key, copy it
Create .env file, write GEMINI_API_KEY=your_key. Add .env to .gitignore — never push keys to GitHub
Create backend/test_gemini.py — send Hello to Gemini and print the response
Study what Gemini returns — the response object, how to extract text, error handling
While loop that takes input, sends to Gemini, prints response, repeats
Pass previous messages to Gemini so it remembers the conversation context
Each member tests 20 questions — note what works and what does not
Write Friday's personality: name, tone, rules, what it should and should not do
Member 4 sketches the chat UI on paper — layout of chat bubbles, input box, send button
Member 3 lists all endpoints needed: /chat, /clear, /status
Run: pip install fastapi uvicorn python-multipart pydantic
Create backend/main.py with basic FastAPI app and health check at /status
POST endpoint that receives a message, sends to Gemini, returns response as JSON
Add CORSMiddleware so React frontend can call backend without errors
Use Pydantic to define ChatRequest and ChatResponse models
Store conversation history in memory per session
All members test the /chat endpoint
Handle: API key missing, Gemini down, empty message, too-long message
Log every request and response to a file for debugging
Member 4 runs npx create-react-app friday-ui and sets up Tailwind CSS

weeks.title: Connect Frontend to Backend — First Full Demo

tasks.detail: Message list, input box, send button, loading spinner
Clean dark theme, chat bubbles, timestamps
Use fetch() or axios to call POST /chat and display response
Show typing indicator while waiting for Gemini's response
Clears conversation history in both UI and backend
All 6 members test the complete flow
2-hour bug fixing session as full team — document all issues
Document how to install and run the project
Record 3-minute screen recording showing Friday having a conversation
30-minute team meeting: what went well, what was hard, what to improve

checkpoint: Working React chat interface at localhost:3000
FastAPI backend running at localhost:8000
Friday responding intelligently to any text message
Multi-turn conversation working
All code committed to GitHub
README that explains how to run the project

months.title: Memory, Personality and LangChain
months.goal: Friday gets a real brain with long-term memory, consistent personality, and smart context management.

tasks.detail: pip install langchain langchain-google-genai langchain-community
Study: LLMs, Chains, Prompts, Memory, Agents — read official docs for 2 hours

tasks.task: Replace Gemini API calls with LangChain
tasks.detail: Use ChatGoogleGenerativeAI from langchain-google-genai
Build Friday's system prompt as a LangChain PromptTemplate with variables
Use LangChain built-in memory to manage short-term conversation history automatically
Combine LLM + Prompt + Memory into a single LangChain ConversationChain
Verify Friday still responds correctly and remembers current conversation
Update FastAPI endpoints to use new LangChain chain
Member 5 updates README with new architecture and setup steps
All members review each other's code — minimum one comment per file
pip install chromadb sentence-transformers tiktoken
Numbers that represent meaning — similar ideas have similar numbers
Create local ChromaDB collection called friday_memory
Write: save_memory(), search_memory(), delete_memory() functions
After each reply ask Gemini to extract user facts and save to ChromaDB
Before each reply search ChromaDB and add relevant memories to context
Close app, reopen, ask Friday what it knows — it should remember
Frontend sidebar showing saved memories with delete button
If new info contradicts old, update instead of duplicating
Data Engineer owns entire vector DB setup

weeks.title: Friday's Personality and System Prompt Engineering

tasks.detail: Member 5 writes 1-page document: name, tone, values, speech style, rules
Convert personality document into detailed system prompt — minimum 300 words
System prompt includes: current date, user name, relevant memories, conversation summary
Ask Friday 100 different questions — check personality stays consistent
Friday greets returning users by name and references their history
Friday politely refuses inappropriate questions — test 20 edge cases
Concise for simple questions, detailed for complex ones
Friday uses bold, bullets, code blocks when appropriate
Member 4 adds react-markdown to render formatted responses
All 6 members test Friday for 30 minutes and write feedback

weeks.title: Smart Context Management

tasks.detail: Gemini can only remember a limited number of words — long conversations hit this limit
When conversation exceeds 10 exchanges, summarize older messages into a paragraph
Include conversation summary in system prompt
Have 50-message conversation — verify it stays coherent throughout
Button to export full conversation as .txt file
Page showing past conversation summaries with dates
Count tokens before sending — truncate if too long
Each browser session gets unique ID — multiple users simultaneously
Record 5-minute demo showing Friday remembering user across two separate sessions
Team meeting: review progress, reassign tasks if anyone behind, plan Month 3

checkpoint: LangChain managing all conversation logic and memory
ChromaDB storing long-term memories that persist across sessions
Friday knowing user's name, preferences, and history
Detailed personality that stays consistent
Smart context management for very long conversations
Conversation summaries and export working

months.title: Voice, Tools and Real-World Capabilities
months.goal: Friday gets ears and a voice. It listens, speaks, searches the web, and reads documents.

tasks.detail: pip install openai-whisper sounddevice soundfile numpy pyaudio
Free speech recognition model by OpenAI — converts audio to text locally
Run whisper.load_model('base') — downloads base model once (~150MB)
Write record_audio.py — records 5 seconds from mic, saves as .wav file
Pass .wav file to Whisper, print transcribed text — test with 20 sentences
Only record when user is speaking — stop when silent for 1 second
Record → transcribe → send to Gemini → display response
Create POST /transcribe endpoint that accepts audio and returns text
Member 4 adds mic button — red when recording, gray when not
All members test with different accents, speeds, and noise levels
pip install gtts playsound — free, basic, robotic voice
Sign up at elevenlabs.io — 10,000 free characters/month, much more natural. Recommended.
Write speak(text) function — converts Friday's response to audio and plays it
If using ElevenLabs, let user choose from 5 different voice options
Split into sentences, play sequentially without gaps
Frontend speaker icon that toggles voice output on and off
Pause, resume, stop buttons for when Friday is speaking
If user speaks while Friday talks, stop playback and process new input
All members listen to 50 responses — rate naturalness 1-10, aim for 7+
Settings slider: 0.5x to 2x playback speed

weeks.title: Tools — Web Search and Document Reading

tasks.detail: Agent lets AI decide which tool to use — study Tools, AgentExecutor, ReAct pattern
pip install duckduckgo-search langchain-community
DuckDuckGo search tool — Friday can now search the web for current info
Safe Python eval() as calculator — Friday can solve math problems
Simple function returning today's date and time
pip install pypdf langchain-text-splitters
User uploads PDF — chunked, embedded, stored in ChromaDB, Friday answers questions about it
Replace ConversationChain with AgentExecutor that has access to all tools
UI shows when Friday uses a tool: Searching the web, Reading document
Test every tool with at least 30 real questions — document and fix failures

weeks.title: Full Voice Pipeline Integration

tasks.detail: Microphone → Whisper → LangChain Agent → Gemini → TTS → Speaker
Target under 3 seconds from end of speech to start of Friday's voice response
Optional: use pvporcupine library for Hey Friday activation
20 complete voice conversations — minimum 10 turns each — fix every issue
Ask Friday to search web, do math, tell time — all via voice only
Background noise, multiple speakers, very fast speech, silence
Measure: average response latency, transcription accuracy, TTS quality rating
No voice pipeline bugs go into Month 4 — dedicated bug-fixing day
Record 7-minute demo: full voice conversation with web search and document reading
Celebrate progress, identify polish needed in Month 4, plan deployment

checkpoint: Voice input — speak to Friday naturally using microphone
Voice output — Friday speaks responses in natural voice
Web search — Friday searches DuckDuckGo for current information
Document reading — Friday reads and answers questions about PDFs
Calculator — Friday solves mathematical problems accurately
Full voice pipeline working end-to-end in under 3 seconds

months.title: Polish, Deploy and Launch
months.goal: Friday goes live on the internet. Polished, fast, secure, and accessible from anywhere.

tasks.detail: Every member uses Friday for 1 hour and writes down every frustration or bug
Member 4 addresses all feedback — aim for UI as clean as ChatGPT
First-time users see: what Friday is, how to use it, Try saying prompts
Voice on/off, voice selection, response speed, memory management
Enter to send, Escape to clear, Ctrl+M for microphone, Ctrl+L to clear chat
Friday works perfectly on a phone browser — many recruiters check mobile
Copy icon on each message to copy text to clipboard
Highlight code responses using react-syntax-highlighter
Switch between dark and light themes — save preference
Keyboard navigation, screen reader labels, color contrast passes WCAG AA
Measure time for each step — find slowest bottleneck
Use Gemini streaming API to display text immediately instead of waiting
Cache common answers so they return instantly
Tiny model for fast transcription, base for accuracy — user choice
Max 20 requests per minute per user to stay within Gemini free tier
API key only on backend — never in frontend code or browser requests
Strip dangerous characters before sending to Gemini
Every possible error shows helpful message — never a blank screen
Simulate 5 simultaneous users — verify no crashes
Member 6 reviews all endpoints for vulnerabilities and exposed secrets
Download Docker Desktop from docker.com
Installs Python, copies code, runs uvicorn on port 8000
Builds React app, serves with nginx on port 80
Runs both backend and frontend with one command: docker-compose up
Verify everything works exactly as in development
Document every environment variable needed
Option A: Render.com (free, easiest). Option B: HuggingFace Spaces (great for AI)
Member 6 creates account on chosen platform, connects GitHub repo
Every push to main branch automatically redeploys Friday
Step-by-step guide so any member can redeploy in under 10 minutes

weeks.title: Go Live and Final Documentation

tasks.detail: Push backend Docker image to Render or HuggingFace — verify /status returns 200
Push React build to Vercel or Netlify — verify it loads and connects to backend
Optional: use Freenom or Namecheap for a free domain

tasks.task: Final end-to-end test on live URL
tasks.detail: Every member tests Friday on live URL for 30 minutes — log every issue
Dedicated 8-hour session to fix every issue found during live testing
10-minute professional demo: text chat, voice, web search, document Q&A
What Friday is, how to use it, how to install locally, team credits, architecture
10-slide deck: problem, solution, demo, tech stack, team, future plans
Member 5 writes Medium or Dev.to article about how Friday was built
Celebrate finishing Path 1. Brief team on Path 2 and who leads what.

checkpoint: Friday is live on the internet with a shareable URL
Voice input and output working on live deployment
Web search, document reading, calculator tools working
Long-term memory persisting across sessions
Mobile responsive on any device
Professional README and demo video on GitHub
10-slide project presentation ready for interviews
Technical blog post published
Docker setup so anyone can run it locally in one command

team_rules: Code is committed to GitHub every working day — no exceptions
Nobody works alone on a problem for more than 2 hours — ask for help
The main branch always works — use feature branches, merge only tested code
No task is done until it is tested with real inputs and edge cases
Document as you build — comments, README updates, docstrings
Weekly demo every Sunday — 30 minutes, working code only, no slides
